name: Viduidae
description: Indigobirds and whydahs are a family of passerine birds endemic to sub-Saharan Africa. They are brood parasites, laying their eggs in the nests of other passerines, and are known for the elaborate tail feathers of breeding males.

children.name: Vidua
children.description: A genus of brood-parasitic finch-like birds with striking sexual dimorphism and long tail feathers in breeding males.

children.name: Vidua macroura
children.description: The Pin-tailed Whydah is a small bird with a black-and-white breeding male that has four long, pin-like tail feathers. It is a brood parasite of waxbills and is found across sub-Saharan Africa.

children.name: Vidua paradisaea
children.description: The Long-tailed Paradise Whydah has a spectacular breeding male with a black back, yellow nape, and extremely elongated tail feathers. It parasitizes green-winged pytilias in East African savannas.

children.name: Vidua chalybeata
children.description: The Village Indigobird is a small, glossy blue-black finch-like bird with a red bill. It is a brood parasite specializing on firefinches and is common across sub-Saharan Africa.

children.name: Vidua obtusa
children.description: The Broad-tailed Paradise Whydah has a breeding male with a broad, spatulate tail and golden-yellow nape. It parasitizes melba finches in woodlands of central and southern Africa.

children.name: Vidua fischeri
children.description: A whydah with straw-coloured tail feathers in the breeding male, found in East Africa.

children.name: Vidua hypocherina
children.description: A steel-blue indigobird found in East Africa.

children.name: Vidua interjecta
children.description: A long-tailed paradise whydah found in West Africa.

children.name: Vidua larvaticola
children.description: An indigobird found in West Africa.

children.name: Vidua maryae
children.description: An indigobird endemic to the Jos Plateau of Nigeria.

children.name: Vidua nigeriae
children.description: An indigobird found in Nigeria and Cameroon.

children.name: Vidua obtusa
children.description: A paradise whydah with broad tail, found in central and southern Africa.

children.name: Vidua orientalis
children.description: An indigobird found in the Sahel region of Africa.

children.name: Vidua purpurascens
children.description: A glossy purple indigobird found in southern Africa.

children.name: Vidua raricola
children.description: An indigobird found in West Africa.

children.name: Vidua regia
children.description: A striking whydah with four long tail feathers ending in spatulate tips, found in southern Africa.

children.name: Vidua togoensis
children.description: A paradise whydah found in West Africa.

children.name: Vidua wilsoni
children.description: An indigobird found in the savannas of West Africa.

children.name: Vidua camerunensis
children.description: An indigobird endemic to Cameroon.

children.name: Vidua funerea
children.description: A dusky indigobird found across much of sub-Saharan Africa.

children.name: Vidua codringtoni
children.description: An indigobird found in eastern Africa.
